title: orobanchaceae
intro: The Orobanchaceae rank refers to the family of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes or Indian paintbrushes. This family is a group of about 200 species of hemiparasitic plants, meaning they obtain some of their nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Orobanchaceae plants are found in many parts of the world and are typically characterized by their unique, often brightly colored flowers. They are often found growing in association with other plants, such as grasses and wildflowers, and can be an important part of their ecosystems.
